mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.83% as of 2024-10-01 reflects recent Fed policy stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 2-month window signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses profit margins and forward earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 708290M and HOUST at 1357K as of 2024-09-01
failure_condition: The interaction effect (joint decline) produces no statistically significant negative alpha relative to single-series signals, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over a 2-month window signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength, which supports profit growth and reduces stagflation risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during INDPRO growth periods are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and core inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed is in restrictive monetary policy stance that constrains credit availability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market prices in prolonged rate persistence.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.83% and CPIAUCSL at 315.631 as of 2024-10-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: The joint condition (high rates AND elevated inflation) produces no statistically significant negative alpha relative to single-series signals, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer income growth and discretionary spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter at an annualized rate below 1.5% signals economic deceleration that compresses forward earnings multiples and growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite weak GDP growth signal, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) over a 2-month window, combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE), signals broad-based demand strength and labor market tightness that supports equity valuations through higher expected earnings and lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase > 2% month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1357K, RSAFS at 708290M, UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2024-10-01
regime_hint: synchronized demand and labor market expansion
failure_condition: The three-way interaction produces no statistically significant positive alpha relative to single-series or pairwise signals, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of co-triggered instances.
